description: Land along the edge of a sea, lake, river, or other body of water is 'kerime'. Beside the sea, the word can cover the coast as well as the immediate waterline. 'leru' (beach) is the part of a shore covered by sand, pebbles, or similar loose material.
articulatory_notes: Make unvoiced 'k' by closing the back of the tongue against the soft palate, then release into middle-height front 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed high-front 'i'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry voice through the nose, and finish with middle-height front 'e'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A shore can be a landing place or route, as well as fishing ground and flood margin. It may also be the first warning line. Its practical shape changes with season, tide, wind, and the water body beside it.

semantic_domains.nature: land directly adjoining a body of water
semantic_domains.spatial: the shifting edge and wider coastal strip between land and water
